leads.name: Spuyten Duyvil
leads.notes: Prolific Brooklyn-based independent press publishing experimental poetry, prose, and hybrid work with open year-round submissions and no reading fee. Steady 15-20 title annual output confirmed through 2024-2025. Strong fit for Cushing-Malloy short-run offset and digital services.
leads.pitch: Active experimental poetry and prose press with consistent annual output and open submissions -- a strong fit for Cushing-Malloy's short-run capabilities.

leads.name: Slant Books
leads.notes: CLMP member literary press in Seattle, WA with a distinguished poetry, fiction, and cultural criticism list. Recent titles by Eric Pankey, Baron Wormser, Scott Cairns, and Gary Fincke. Consistent 10-12 titles per year. Publisher Gregory Wolfe is a well-known figure in literary publishing.
leads.pitch: Respected CLMP member literary press with a distinguished poetry list and steady 10-12 title annual output.

leads.name: Sagging Meniscus Press
leads.notes: Distinctive independent press in Montclair, NJ specializing in experimental fiction, philosophy-adjacent literature, and what they call 'misfit text.' Consistent 10-15 title annual output confirmed through 2024-2025 (Peel Me, A Wretched Thing of Beauty). No named contact publicly listed; outreach goes to general info address.
leads.pitch: Experimental literary press with a strong 10-15 title annual output and a distinctive niche that aligns well with Cushing-Malloy's short-run manufacturing expertise.

leads.name: The Song Cave
leads.notes: One of the most respected small poetry presses in the country. Recent titles by Deborah Landau, Mei-Mei Berssenbrugge, Brenda Hillman, and Madeline ffitch. Output of 6-10 titles per year is modest but consistent, and the press's design-forward aesthetic makes production quality a top priority. Co-founded by Ben Estes and Alan Felsenthal in Brooklyn.
leads.pitch: Prestigious poetry press publishing major American poets -- production quality is central to their brand, making them an ideal Cushing-Malloy fit.

leads.name: Fernwood Press
leads.notes: Newly independent press led by Michael Wiegers, longtime executive editor of Copper Canyon Press. 2024-2025 titles include Ilya Kaminsky, Laura Kasischke, Ellen Bass, Camille Dungy, and Linda Bierds -- a debut list that rivals any poetry publisher in the country. Located in Port Townsend, WA. A flagship account opportunity for Cushing-Malloy.
leads.pitch: New press led by a legendary poetry editor with a debut list including Ilya Kaminsky and Ellen Bass -- the highest-profile new poetry press of the decade.

leads.name: Autofocus Books
leads.notes: Very active memoir, essay, and autofiction press with 12+ confirmed titles in 2024-2025. Midwest-based editor Michael Wheaton. No public email listed on site; info@ is a best-available domain-pattern fallback -- recommend verifying via contact form. Focus skews CNF and memoir rather than poetry, but falls squarely within literary publishing.
leads.pitch: High-output literary CNF and memoir press with 12 or more titles per year -- a strong candidate for Cushing-Malloy's digital short-run capabilities.

leads.name: Green Linden Press
leads.notes: Small independent poetry and fiction press in Hagerstown, MD with a working-class literary sensibility. Recent titles: Winter Fires, Psalms of Innocence, dementia lyrics. Publisher Charles Rammelkamp. Direct confirmed email and named publisher make outreach straightforward. Output of 5-8 titles per year is consistent.
leads.pitch: Independent poetry and literary fiction press with a distinctive working-class voice and a direct named contact -- a clean, straightforward outreach target.

leads.name: Circumference Books
leads.notes: Highly regarded dual-language poetry translation press in Brooklyn, NY. 2024-2025 titles include Inger Christensen, Tanella Boni, Nuno Judice, and Nathalie Quintane. Co-founded by Anna Moschovakis (poet and translator). Dual-language format requires precise typesetting and premium production -- a natural fit for Cushing-Malloy's quality focus.
leads.pitch: Prestigious dual-language poetry translation press whose format-intensive books demand the precision manufacturing Cushing-Malloy delivers.

leads.name: Two Lines Press
leads.source: Center for the Art of Translation / press website
leads.notes: The book publishing arm of the Center for the Art of Translation in San Francisco. Publishes world literature in translation exclusively -- 10+ confirmed titles in 2024-2025. Consistent 10-15 title output, institutional backing, and a nationally distributed list. Publisher Olivia Taylor Smith.
leads.pitch: Institutionally backed world literature press with a consistent 10-15 title annual output and a clear identity as one of the premier translation publishers in the country.

leads.name: Wave Books
leads.notes: One of the premier independent poetry publishers in the country, based in Seattle, WA. Founded 2005. 10-12 titles per year of poetry and prose. Publisher Charlie Wright. Strong national distribution through Consortium. Titles by Matthew Zapruder, James Tate, Eleni Sikelianos, CAConrad. Design-forward press with a reputation for exceptional physical production.
leads.pitch: Premier independent poetry publisher in Seattle with a 20-year track record, nationally distributed catalog, and a strong commitment to the physical quality of every book.

leads.name: 3: A Taos Press
leads.notes: Poetry-exclusive press in Denver, CO with invitation-only submissions and a prestigious author roster. Upcoming titles include Cyrus Cassells (2026) and Seth Brady Tucker (2025). Publishing 4-6 titles per year. Named publisher Andrea L. Watson with confirmed email and phone. Southwest regional press with a national reputation.
leads.pitch: Invitation-only poetry press with a prestigious author roster and confirmed contact details -- a clean, high-quality outreach target for Cushing-Malloy.

leads.name: Northwestern University Press
leads.notes: Northwestern University Press's Curbstone Books and TriQuarterly Books imprints publish 4-6 literary titles per year focused on social justice voices, international literature, poetry, and fiction. Director Parneshia Jones is herself a poet with deep ties to the independent literary community. Strong Midwest university press account with confirmed contact. Located in Evanston, IL -- just outside Chicago.
leads.pitch: Major Midwest university press with distinguished literary imprints -- Director Parneshia Jones's background in poetry makes this an especially resonant outreach.

leads.name: University of Arkansas Press
leads.notes: Publisher of the Miller Williams Poetry Prize and the Etel Adnan Poetry Series -- two of the most prestigious poetry series at any university press. Director Mike Bieker. Active 2025 and 2026 prize winners already announced, creating a predictable production pipeline. A university press account with a well-defined poetry series is an ideal Cushing-Malloy relationship.
leads.pitch: University press home to the Miller Williams Poetry Prize -- a prestigious, predictable production pipeline that aligns perfectly with Cushing-Malloy's capabilities.

leads.name: University of Georgia Press
leads.notes: Publisher of the Georgia Poetry Prize and the Flannery O'Connor Award for Short Fiction -- two of the most storied prizes in American literary publishing. Acquisitions Editor Sarah Shermyen. Active 2024-2025 prize winners confirmed. A southeastern university press account with a dual poetry/fiction prize structure.
leads.pitch: Home to the Georgia Poetry Prize and Flannery O'Connor Award -- a prestigious university press with two major literary prize series and confirmed contact details.

leads.name: Bridwell Press
leads.notes: Newly launched poetry press affiliated with Southern Methodist University in Dallas, TX. Three editorial lines with an annual May submissions window. Editor Lauren Brazeal Garza; founding director David Caplan is a respected poet-critic. Output of 2-4 titles per year at launch. A developing-account target where an early relationship could grow as the press scales.
leads.pitch: Newly launched SMU-affiliated poetry press building its inaugural list -- an early-mover opportunity for Cushing-Malloy to establish a long-term relationship.

leads.name: Pelekinesis
leads.notes: Active independent press in Claremont, CA publishing flash fiction, poetry, experimental work, and the Best Microfiction anthology series -- confirmed active through mid-2025. Publisher Mark Givens. Direct phone and email confirmed on site. 8-12 annual titles. The Best Microfiction series represents a high-profile recurring title with predictable annual production needs.
leads.pitch: Active independent press with confirmed contact details, a recurring anthology series, and 8-12 annual titles -- a solid mid-tier Cushing-Malloy prospect.

leads.name: University of New Mexico Press
leads.notes: Publisher of the Mary Burritt Christiansen Poetry Series with confirmed 2024-2025 titles. Director Stephen Hull. Also publishes literary CNF and fiction. A Southwest university press with a named, active poetry series and institutional stability. Using general press director as contact since prior series acquisitions editor could not be confirmed as current.
leads.pitch: Southwest university press with a named, active poetry series -- a steady, professionally operated account well-suited to Cushing-Malloy's university press capabilities.

leads.name: Astrophil Press
leads.notes: University of South Dakota-affiliated poetry and hybrid press. Confirmed active with SISYPHUSINA (Shira Dentz, April 2025) and Harvestman (May 2024). No direct contact info publicly listed -- About page returns 404, no named editor found. Outreach via submissions@ address. Flag as low contact-confidence but strong literary fit if reachable.
leads.pitch: Active university-affiliated poetry and hybrid press with 2024-2025 titles confirmed -- worth pursuing despite limited public contact information.

leads.name: BOA Editions
leads.notes: One of the most celebrated poetry publishers in the United States. Rochester, NY. Founded 1976. Publisher Peter Conners. 10-14 titles per year across poetry, short prose, and translations. Home to the American Poets Continuum Series and Lannan Translations Selection. Distributed by Consortium. Major prize winners throughout catalog. A flagship account opportunity for Cushing-Malloy.
leads.pitch: One of the premier independent poetry publishers in the country -- 10 to 14 titles per year, Consortium distribution, and a 50-year track record in Rochester, NY.

leads.name: Copper Canyon Press
leads.notes: One of the most celebrated poetry publishers in the country. Port Townsend, WA. Founded 1972. 14-18 titles per year. Recent titles by Ocean Vuong, Naomi Shihab Nye, Juan Felipe Herrera, Carolyn Forche. Distributed by Consortium. Executive Director George Knotek. Long-term Lannan Literary Foundation partner. A flagship account for any book manufacturer.
leads.pitch: One of the most celebrated poetry publishers in America with a 50-year track record, a major national author roster, and 14-18 titles per year.
